bullets: PP fell sharply across the region on persistent oversupply and weakening demand, with producers cutting to close orders before prices slip further.
A deepening flood of cheap Korean cargo — including deeply-discounted off-grade material — keeps undercutting Chinese and domestic supply.
Chinese offers have turned sparse and uncompetitive, leaving Korean and domestic cargo to set the market.
PE led the market sharply lower today as crude and naphtha weakness and soft seasonal demand drove broad cuts across HDPE, LLDPE and LDPE.
Very cheap US cargo is resetting expectations lower, though distant arrival keeps buyers hesitant for now.
More downside is expected once the Strait of Hormuz reopens; buyers are holding to need-based purchasing.
PVC held flat today, steadying after yesterday's sharp domestic cut, but sentiment stays fragile.
Regional benchmarks are unchanged on soft demand — a stability the market sees as weak rather than firm.
Southern participants expect further erosion toward the 20,000 VND/kg mark next month if demand stays soft.

summary: Platts NE Asia: HDPE -90, LDPE -65, LLDPE -25 on week; SEA HDPE -50 to 1,180-1,190 CFR.
US Exxon LLDPE Film offered to Vietnam at 930 CIF (close possible at 900) — lowest, but Sept-Oct arrival.
Sabic settled LDPE Film 1,300 / coating 1,400 CIF; domestic LDPE/LLDPE/HDPE all cut ~500-1,000 on week.
paras: According to Platts pricing published today, June 25, in Northeast Asia, the price level for HDPE film/yarn/blow/injection grades fell sharply this week by up to USD 90/ton versus the previous week; LLDPE fell USD 25/ton; LDPE fell USD 65/ton. In Southeast Asia, the price level for all HDPE grades likewise dropped USD 50/ton versus the previous week, fluctuating between USD 1,180–1,190/ton; LLDPE fell USD 20/ton to USD 1,170/ton/CFR, LLDPE metallocene C6 fell USD 20/ton to USD 1,320/ton/CFR; LDPE fell USD 20/ton to USD 1,320/ton/CFR.

The Asian PE market continued its downward trend this week under pressure from the decline in the crude oil and naphtha markets, combined with subdued seasonal demand. In Southeast Asia, most market participants maintained a wait-and-see attitude, with purchasing mainly driven by actual needs, amid expectations that prices will continue to be adjusted downward once the Strait of Hormuz reopens.

In Vietnam, the market recorded several offers for US-origin cargoes; many traders considered these prices very competitive, possibly due to selling pressure from high inventories in the US and long shipping times.

For example, today a distributor offered LLDPE Film 2018XBU/2018AY of US-origin Exxon Mobil to Vietnam at USD 930/ton/CIF Ho Chi Minh City. The cargo is loading in July, arriving in Vietnam around September–October 2026, payment by LC or TT 20/80. One distributor said the supplier could close this sale at USD 900/ton/CIF. This is the lowest LLDPE price at present; however, the delivery is rather distant, so buyers remain hesitant.

Late this morning, the distributor of Saudi Arabia's Sabic announced a closing sale price for an LDPE cargo for July 2026 delivery to Vietnamese buyers, CIF from Singapore. Accordingly, Sabic's closing sale price for LDPE Film was USD 1,300/ton/CIF, exactly equal to the bid taken at the start of the week. Sabic's closing sale price for LDPE coating was USD 1,400/ton/CIF, USD 60/ton lower than the bid taken at the start of the week. In addition to CIF cargo from Singapore, the distributor also announced a closing sale of LDPE coating 7019 EC at the Binh Duong bonded warehouse at USD 1,420/ton/CIF. Sabic's closing sale prices for LLDPE and HDPE this morning have not yet been announced.
In domestic trading, the PE market continued to decline versus the start of the week. A
northern seller lowered the price of Lotte Titan Malaysia's LDPE 260GG to VND 44,000/kg including VAT – down VND 500/kg versus the start of the week, yet buyers remained unenthusiastic.

For LLDPE, a major distributor lowered its offer for Sabic's LLDPE Film 218WJ to VND 33,000/kg including VAT, also down VND 500/kg versus the container offer price at the start of the week. Russia's Sibur LLDPE Film was still offered at VND 32,000/kg including VAT at Hai Phong port, while some sellers lowered their offers for China-origin LLDPE Film below this level – although at the start of the week they were still offering from VND 33,000/kg including VAT and up.

As for HDPE, a distributor lowered its offer for Qamar's HDPE Film F1 to VND 36,000/kg including VAT, up to VND 1,000–2,000/kg lower than offers from some other traders, even though HDPE F1 inventories are not large and the cost of imported stock is high…

summary: Platts SEA PP yarn/inj -95 to 1,095 CFR, BOPP -95 to 1,115, IPP/copo -95 to 1,145 on regional oversupply.
Korean flood deepens: Hanwha HJ500 990 CIF; off-grade HJ500Z sold domestically at just 28,000 incl VAT.
China offers sparse and uncompetitive vs Korean/domestic; NSRP 114G closing 32,000-32,200 incl VAT.
paras: According to Platts pricing published today, June 25, in Northeast Asia, the price level for PP yarn/injection fell sharply this week by up to USD 90/ton versus the previous week, IPP Film fell USD 125/ton, BOPP fell USD 130/ton. In Southeast Asia, the price level for PP yarn/injection this week also fell sharply by up to USD 95/ton to USD 1,095/ton/CFR, BOPP fell USD 95/ton to USD 1,115/ton/CFR, IPP Film and block copolymer fell USD 95/ton to USD 1,145/ton/CFR.

Thus, Asian PP prices fell very sharply this week amid weakening purchasing demand across the region. Market participants reported an ongoing oversupply situation, while buyers were wary and limited their orders as prices fell continuously day by day throughout the week. Producers also proactively offered lower prices to close orders before the market declined further. In addition, the appearance of competitively priced cargoes from South Korea flowing into Southeast Asia made Chinese suppliers hesitant to cut prices further to compete.

In Vietnam, traders distributing China-origin cargo all shared that China's PP export offer prices fell deeply. Even so, offers of China-origin PP to Vietnam this week were rather sparse; many distributors mainly offered to a few traditional customers who could close orders, rather than offering widely. The reason is that China-origin PP offer prices remained higher than Korean cargo and domestic PP in Vietnam, so distributors were unenthusiastic about offering, knowing their own offer prices would struggle to be accepted by the market…

Today, many market participants were still waiting for Sabic's PP closing sale price, with cargo delivered from the S-Oil plant in South Korea. Some sources said an offer of PP injection HJ500 from Hanwha Total of South Korea to Vietnam was at USD 990/ton/CIF; an offer of PP block copolymer BJ300 from Hanwha at USD 1,020/ton/CIF at the start of this week attracted significant buyer interest and achieved a certain volume of transactions.

Domestically, a trader offered PP HJ500 at just VND 30,000/kg including VAT for a cargo arriving in early July, willing to negotiate further. This price is about VND 2,000/kg cheaper than PP homo of Vietnam's NSRP origin, Chinese cargo or Russian cargo.

In addition, some traders also offered Hanwha's PP HJ500Z (off-grade) at only about VND 28,000–28,500/kg including VAT. A northern trader shared that they had sold nearly 300 tons of HJ500Z at VND 28,000/kg including VAT, with delivery in early July…
Hanwha's PP copolymer BJ300, BJ350, BJ550 were also offered at around VND 33,000–34,000/kg including VAT, lower than the domestic PP copolymer offers of Hyosung or other origins.

For domestic cargo, at the start of this week, NSRP's PP 114G offer was lowest at VND 32,500/kg including VAT; the lowest closing transaction price was VND 32,000–32,200/kg including VAT depending on volume. This price was down VND 300–500/kg versus the previous week.

In the south, the Hyosung Vina plant offered PP injection J700N at VND 32,000/kg excluding VAT (with the full 8% VAT, around VND 34,560/kg including VAT); offered PP block copolymer J640N at VND 35,000/kg excluding VAT (with the full 8% VAT, around VND 37,800/kg including VAT); offered PP random R701N at VND 43,000/kg excluding VAT (with the full 8% VAT, around VND 46,440/kg including VAT)…

summary: Platts flat: China 750 / SEA 725 CFR — a 'weak stability' with soft downstream demand.
Domestic flat today after yesterday's deep AGC Vina PM66R cut to 20,900-21,000 incl VAT.
China operating rate up to ~70%; talk of base prices retreating toward 20,000 incl VAT next month.
paras: This week China's overall PVC capacity utilization rate reached 70%, up slightly versus the previous week. The operating rate at carbide-based plants in the Northwest rose slightly, with sporadic restarts of units that had previously been idling. The operating rate of ethylene-based PVC remained low, at around 50%. Some maintenance units in coastal regions gradually resumed operation and the ethylene-based operating rate held steady without further declines, although it remained at historic lows.

On price developments, according to Platts pricing, the price level for PVC in Asia over the past week generally maintained a stable state. Specifically, PVC CFR China prices were flat at USD 750/ton, while PVC CFR Southeast Asia prices held at USD 725/ton.

However, this stability is a “weak stability”, reflecting the reality that the market has not seen a clear improvement in supply and demand. Downstream demand remained low, while trading activity was subdued as buyers continued to be cautious, mainly purchasing according to actual needs and expecting prices may still fluctuate further.

In the domestic market, after yesterday's sharp decline, when some southern traders deeply lowered offers for AGC Vina's (ACVN) PVC PM66R to the VND 20,900–21,000/kg range (VAT included), about VND 1,000/kg lower than the mid-month offer from the plant (VND 20,400–20,600/kg, VAT excluded), the domestic PVC market today was generally flat.

However, the price level still faces considerable pressure after this strong adjustment. The deep cut in domestic factory prices has spread negative sentiment, increasing pressure on the entire domestic PVC market.

Some market participants in the southern region believe the downward trend has not yet ended, and that base PVC prices in the coming month may continue to retreat toward around VND 20,000/kg (VAT included) if demand does not improve markedly.

On imports, at the start of this week, a distributor announced a sale price for US Shintech's PVC SE950/SE1000 to Vietnamese buyers at USD 670/ton/CIF, distant delivery, expected to arrive in the country in October 2026. Previously, in mid-month, Shintech's PVC closing sale price for a cargo expected to arrive in September was USD 690/ton/CIF. Thus, this week's price fell a further USD 20/ton.
